data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **`.csproj` changes:**
   - Replace `xunit` (v2) with `xunit.v3` (v3)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` — xUnit v3 doesn't need a separate runner package; tests are self-executing
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` — not needed for xUnit v3 (tests compile to executables)
   - Remove `Xunit.SkippableFact` — xUnit v3 has built-in skip support via `Skip` property on `[Fact]`/`[Theory]`
   - Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` since xUnit v3 test projects compile to executables

2. **`ConditionalTests.cs` changes:**
   - Replace `[SkippableFact]` and `[SkippableTheory]` with `[Fact]` and `[Theory]`, using `Assert.Skip()` instead of the `Skip.IfNot()` and `Skip.If()` methods
   - Keep the `using Xunit;` namespace as-is

3. **`global.json` changes:**
   - Update the test runner from `"VSTest"` to `"Microsoft.Testing.Platform"`, which is the default for xUnit v3
   - The xUnit v3 package on NuGet is `xunit.v3`, and it automatically generates an entry point, so I don't need to manually add one

Now I'll apply these migration steps.

</Project>

data.content: The .NET 11 preview SDK has a protocol mismatch with `dotnet test`. Since xUnit v3 projects are self-executable, let me run the tests directly:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run xUnit v3 tests as executable

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run xUnit v3 tests as executable

data.reasoningText:
This seems to be a compatibility issue between the .NET 11 preview SDK and xUnit v3's test runner protocol. The `dotnet test` command's new testing platform runner isn't compatible. Let me try running the test executable directly, which is the xUnit v3 way, or try with the VSTest runner in global.json.

Actually, let me try running the executable directly since xUnit v3 test projects compile to executables:

arguments.description: Run xUnit v3 tests as executable

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-3e61e405bb434a55a0c6665a26ff0d55 && dotnet run 2>&1", "description": "Run xUnit v3 tests as executable", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.description: Run xUnit v3 tests as executable

data.content: All 3 tests ran successfully (skipped as expected on this Linux environment). Let me show the final state of all files:

Migration complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`TestProject.csproj`**
- Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` (v3 tests are self-executable)
- Replaced `xunit`, `xunit.runner.visualstudio`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` → single `xunit.v3` package
- Removed `Xunit.SkippableFact` (skip is built into v3)

**`ConditionalTests.cs`**
- `[SkippableFact]` → `[Fact]` with `Assert.SkipUnless()`
- `[SkippableTheory]` → `[Theory]` with `Assert.SkipWhen()`

**`global.json`**
- Test runner changed from `VSTest` → `Microsoft.Testing.Platform`
